payload.answer: Only one low-confidence, attributed note should be kept. Do not store three copies, and do not convert this into a durable fact that Sam likes Bill Evans because the pronoun 'he' is ambiguous and the entities are not uniquely resolved.
citations: Same input delivered three times - Sam from Goldman mentioned that he likes Bill Evans

entity_resolution.reason: Neither Sam, Goldman, nor Bill Evans is uniquely identified, and the pronoun 'he' is ambiguous.

memory_cards.statement: Sam from Goldman mentioned that 'he likes Bill Evans'; the referent of 'he' is unclear, so this should not be stored as a resolved preference fact.

details: Detected repeated delivery of the same message and deduplicated it to a single candidate memory.
Preserved ambiguity by storing an attributed mention instead of a resolved preference fact.
Avoided asserting any uniquely identified entity linkage beyond the textual mention.

repair_options: Specify which Sam and which Goldman organization you mean.
Specify which Bill Evans you mean.
